events.title: Strait of Hormuz geopolitical uncertainty impacts oil flows
events.description: Lingering geopolitical uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz continues to affect oil markets, despite OPEC+ plans to increase output. The situation remains a key risk to global energy supply chains.

events.title: Saudi Arabia increases oil shipments via Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Saudi Arabia has increased oil exports through the Strait of Hormuz following the U.S.-Iran agreement to reopen the sea lane. According to Kpler, about 34 million barrels have been shipped through the strait since June 17.

events.title: Tianjin Port sets new draft record with ultra-large bulk carrier
events.description: Tianjin Port in China accommodated the ultra-large bulk carrier Zhonghai Ronghua with a draft of 22.18 meters, setting a new record for the deepest-draft vessel to dock at the port.

events.title: Middle East tensions impact Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Despite a recent 60-day peace agreement, the situation in the Middle East remains fragile, with US and Iran exchanging strikes. Freight flow through the Strait of Hormuz is increasing post-attacks but remains disrupted.

events.title: EU RED III policy concerns in West Med bunker markets
events.description: Gibraltar Port Authority is concerned about the impact of the EU's Renewable Energy Directive III on West Mediterranean bunker markets, potentially shifting refueling to non-European ports with limited decarbonization benefits.

events.title: Israeli Defense Ministry opposes Zim sale to Hapag-Lloyd
events.description: Israel's Defense Ministry has formally opposed the proposed $4.2 billion sale of Zim to Hapag-Lloyd, citing concerns over maritime security and supply chain impacts. The move adds to government scrutiny of the transaction.

events.title: ONE launches Mediterranean Africa Express service
events.description: Ocean Network Express (ONE) has launched the Mediterranean Africa Express (MAX) service, a weekly route connecting West Africa to global trade routes, starting July 7, 2026. The service aims to enhance direct freight links between West Africa and major global markets.

events.title: Maersk resumes Melbourne Star seasonal export service
events.description: Maersk is resuming its seasonal Melbourne Star export service to support Australia's peak export season, with weekly calls at DP World Terminal Melbourne and connections to Southeast Asian transshipment hubs.

events.title: Dedicated cargo flights to Chicago launched
events.description: Ceva, DSV, and Kuehne+Nagel have launched dedicated international cargo flights to the Chicago area through agreements with major cargo airlines to enhance freight services.

events.title: Potential Suez Canal shipping return due to Persian Gulf peace deal
events.description: A major carrier partnership may shift global shipping back to the Suez Canal following a peace deal in the Persian Gulf, signaling a potential easing of route disruptions.

events.title: Asia-Europe ocean freight rate split nears record
events.description: Disruption at the Strait of Hormuz has caused a near-record pricing gap between Asia-North Europe and Asia-Mediterranean container services, distorting global freight markets.

events.title: Fatal accident halts JNPA Nhava Sheva terminal operations
events.description: Operations at Nhava Sheva Freeport Terminal in India’s JNPA port were halted following a fatal accident involving container collapse during sludge removal. The terminal is expected to remain closed until tomorrow.

events.title: Jeddah port congestion and Suez Canal service update
events.description: Jeddah port continues to experience 4-6 hour delays, impacting shipments. Meanwhile, the Gemini carriers, including Maersk, have resumed the AE15 service through the Suez Canal, signaling potential easing of regional freight bottlenecks.

events.title: Israeli officials block ZIM sale to Hapag-Lloyd
events.description: Israeli government officials, including Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, have opposed ZIM Integrated Shipping Services' proposed sale to Hapag-Lloyd, citing strategic concerns over Qatari and Saudi ownership stakes. The decision has caused ZIM's stock to drop significantly.

events.title: Maersk resumes Suez route for AE15 service
events.description: Maersk, in collaboration with Hapag-Lloyd, announced a return to the trans-Suez route for the AE15 service, following security assessments in the Red Sea. This marks a gradual shift back from the Cape of Good Hope route.

events.title: Indonesia and Singapore reaffirm Strait of Malacca free passage
events.description: Indonesia and Singapore have reaffirmed their commitment to maintaining free passage through the Strait of Malacca amid renewed concerns over global navigation security due to Middle East tensions.

events.title: AE15 service resumes Suez Canal route
events.description: Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd are resuming container shipping through the Suez Canal with their AE15 service, marking a cautious return after previously using the Cape of Good Hope route.

events.title: 44 Seafarers Held by Somali Pirates
events.description: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) has called for urgent action to secure the release of 44 seafarers held captive by pirates in Somali waters, highlighting a critical security threat to maritime operations.

events.title: Tank Barge Explosion on Paraguay River
events.description: A tank barge exploded on the Paraguay River, killing five workers. The incident occurred on Saturday morning and could impact river freight operations.

events.title: CMA CGM adds peak season surcharges on key trade lanes
events.description: CMA CGM announced new Peak Season Surcharges (PSS) of $500 per container on trade lanes from the Indian Subcontinent and China, effective July 2026, impacting routes to Latin America and other destinations.

events.title: Shipping vulnerability in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Industry sentiment highlights ongoing vulnerability of commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, despite recent diplomatic efforts. The latest incident reinforces concerns about potential disruptions to global freight flows in the region.

events.title: CNC launches direct Shantou–Thailand/Cambodia route
events.description: CNC has revamped its JTX service to include the first direct connection from Shantou to Thailand and Cambodia, enhancing Intra-Asia freight connectivity and offering a new route option between China and Southeast Asia.

events.title: Tehran-linked ship grounded in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A 2006-built 1,574 TEU container ship, the Arista, which is linked to Iran and sanctioned by the US, ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz on 1 July 2026. The incident could disrupt critical shipping flows through this key global chokepoint.

events.title: Cargo vessel attacked off Yemen in Red Sea
events.description: A cargo vessel was attacked by unknown armed assailants approximately 30 nautical miles off Yemen in the Red Sea, raising concerns about ongoing maritime security risks in the region.

events.title: Panama Canal reduces neopanamax draught limits
events.description: The Panama Canal Authority is reducing the maximum authorised draught for neopanamax vessels to 14.94 meters, signaling renewed water scarcity concerns at this critical shipping chokepoint.

events.title: US courts redefine 'package' under COGSA
events.description: Recent US court decisions, particularly in the Second Circuit, are shifting the interpretation of 'package' under COGSA, prioritizing contractual definitions in bills of lading. This could significantly impact liability limits for carriers and affect claims handling in the shipping industry.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz vessel transits drop to four-day low
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz fell to a four-day low on July 2 due to ongoing regional tensions. Iran-linked vessels accounted for nearly 60% of crossings, according to S&P Global Energy vessel tracking data.

events.title: Russia's crude export flow shift impacts tanker market
events.description: Russia’s reduced crude processing capacity is increasing seaborne exports, altering cargo flows and impacting the tanker market as the Ukraine war enters its fifth year.

events.title: VLCC freight market shifts due to geopolitical risks
events.description: The VLCC freight market is transitioning from pricing based on risk to pricing based on vessel utilisation, as geopolitical tensions persist in the Strait of Hormuz. This shift reflects ongoing uncertainty and evolving dynamics in global oil shipping routes.

events.title: Cargo ship attacked near Hodeidah, Yemen
events.description: A cargo ship reported an attack off the coast of Yemen near Hodeidah, where hostilities between Houthi rebels and international forces continue.

events.title: Port of Galveston opens new cargo berth
events.description: The Port of Galveston opened a new cargo berth with the arrival of its first Wallenius Wilhelmsen cargo vessel on July 2, 2026, expanding its capacity for freight operations.

events.title: Sailors Stranded Behind Hormuz Due To Conflict
events.description: Over 8,000 sailors remain stranded behind the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing disruptions, likely due to geopolitical tensions. The situation highlights a critical chokepoint disruption affecting global shipping and crew movements.

events.title: Cargo Ship Attacked Off Yemen
events.description: A cargo vessel in the Red Sea reported an attack by armed assailants, highlighting ongoing security risks in the region and adding to supply chain disruptions.

events.title: European Hormuz Naval Coalition Disbands
events.description: The European-led naval coalition, established to protect shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, has disbanded without formal notice, raising concerns about regional security and potential disruptions to global freight flows.

events.title: Hannibal launches Melzo–Rotterdam intermodal rail service
events.description: Hannibal, a Contship Group operator, has launched a new intermodal rail service between Melzo (Rail Hub Milano) and Rotterdam Europoort, enhancing freight connectivity and access to one of Europe's largest logistics hubs.

events.title: Red Sea risk persists despite freight recovery
events.description: Freight flows in the Hormuz corridor are recovering, but risks in the Red Sea remain, highlighting ongoing safety concerns despite improved traffic conditions.

events.title: CMA CGM increases Asia-Europe-North Africa FAK rates
events.location: Asia to Europe and North Africa
events.description: CMA CGM has announced increased Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for container shipments from Asia to North Europe, the Mediterranean, and North Africa, effective from 15 July to 31 July 2026. The rate changes include $4,100 for 20-foot containers to North Europe.

events.title: Iran and Qatar resume maritime trade
events.description: Maritime trade between Iran and Qatar has resumed after a five-month suspension, following an interim deal between the two countries. The resumption could impact regional freight flows and trade dynamics in the Persian Gulf.

events.title: Rising Violence Against Truckers in Mexico Borderlands
events.description: While cargo theft in the Mexico Borderlands has decreased, attacks on truckers have become deadlier, raising concerns about safety and security for freight operations in the region.

events.title: CMA CGM launches Iberimar shipping service
events.description: CMA CGM has launched the Iberimar service, a weekly shipping route connecting the Iberian Peninsula's Atlantic coast with Morocco and the Strait of Gibraltar. The service includes calls at Tangier, Algeciras, Vigo, Leixões, Lisbon, Casablanca, and Agadir.

events.title: KMTC merges JKW3 and JKW4 services
events.description: KMTC is consolidating its fortnightly JKW3 and JKW4 Busan-Japan services into a single weekly JKW3 loop. The change removes Nagasaki from the rotation and adjusts port frequencies.

events.title: Diamond Line launches Spain-Morocco feeder service
events.description: Diamond Line has launched a new Spain-Morocco shuttle service to enhance cargo links between Valencia and Casablanca, operating on a fixed seven-day schedule.

events.title: Akkon Lines expands EAS service to Mogadishu
events.description: Akkon Lines has added Mogadishu, Somalia, to its East Africa–Indian Subcontinent (EAS) service, operating fortnightly with 1,700 TEU vessels. The route now includes Nhava Sheva, Mundra, Karachi, Dar es Salaam, Mombasa, and Mogadishu.

events.title: CMA CGM Ship Damaged by Missile in Hormuz Strait
events.description: A CMA CGM container ship was struck by a missile in the Strait of Hormuz in early May, sustaining severe damage that may lead to its scrapping.

events.title: Ukrainian drone attack hits St Petersburg oil terminal
events.description: A major Ukrainian drone attack hit a port and oil infrastructure in St Petersburg, Russia, causing supply chain disruptions to oil logistics.

events.title: Maersk raises ECS for Indian Subcontinent to West Coast Latin America
events.location: Indian Subcontinent to West Coast Latin America
events.description: Maersk has increased Emergency Contingency Surcharges (ECS) for shipments from the Indian Subcontinent, including North West India, Pakistan, South and East India, Bangladesh, Sri Lanka, and the Maldives, to the West Coast of Latin America, the Caribbean, and Central America.

events.title: Ruscon uses Northern Sea Route for China to St. Petersburg
events.description: Ruscon, part of the Delo Group, is utilizing the Northern Sea Route for westbound container shipments from China to St. Petersburg, reducing transit times to around 30 days compared to the Suez Canal route.

events.title: Gulftainer launches Umm Qasr feeder service
events.description: Gulftainer has launched a dedicated feeder service connecting the UAE and Umm Qasr, Iraq, through its shipping arm GT Lines. This marks an expansion of Gulftainer's integrated logistics operations in the region.

events.title: Middle East tanker flows recover post-ceasefire
events.description: In June, the Middle East tanker market showed cautious recovery as a temporary US-Iran ceasefire boosted regional transit activity, according to Gibson Shipbrokers Ltd.

events.title: Iran warns oil tankers on Hormuz routes
events.description: Iran’s joint military command warned oil tankers to use approved routes in the Strait of Hormuz or face a 'forceful response,' increasing tensions in a key global energy supply route.

events.title: Gulf of Hormuz Fleet Movement and Mine Constraints
events.description: Over 550 merchant vessels are preparing to exit the Gulf of Hormuz, with crude flows exceeding 10 million barrels per day. The central shipping channel remains constrained by an estimated 80 mines.

events.title: Intra-Asia container freight rates decline
events.description: Drewry's Intra-Asia Container Index fell 4% last week, with notable declines on routes like Shanghai–Busan and Ho Chi Minh City. This follows a second consecutive week of rate reductions, indicating easing demand during the early peak season.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Impacts Shipping
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, placing shipping at the 'frontline' of geo-political tensions and disrupting global freight flows.

events.title: Asia-US container rates surge due to impending tariffs
events.description: Container shipping rates from Asia to the US West Coast have surged by 8% as importers rush to move cargo ahead of potential tariff increases. This rate increase reflects trade policy uncertainty and its impact on freight flows.

events.title: Port of Galveston opens new cargo berth
events.description: The Port of Galveston officially opened a new cargo berth on July 2, with the first call by a Wallenius Wilhelmsen cargo carrier. The development supports increased roll-on/roll-off cargo operations at the port.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd increases freight rates for India/Pakistan to Europe
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has raised ocean freight rates for shipments from India and Pakistan to Northern Europe and the Mediterranean, effective from 1 August 2026. The base rate for 20-foot dry containers from Nhava Sheva and Mundra will increase by US$2,000 per container.

events.title: Emirates deploys first Boeing 777-300ERSF freighter
events.description: Emirates has deployed its first converted Boeing 777-300ERSF freighter, registered A6-EBK, into commercial service. The aircraft carried over 100 tonnes of cargo on its inaugural flight from Hong Kong to Dubai, expanding the airline's cargo capabilities.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Security Concerns
events.description: Vessel transits through the Strait of Hormuz have increased, but uncertainty remains over the security of safe passage amid ongoing geopolitical tensions and US-Iran peace talks.

events.title: Container rates rise sharply on transpacific and Asia-Europe routes
events.description: The Drewry World Container Index increased by 9% to $4,530 per 40ft container, driven by rising spot rates on transpacific and Asia-Europe trades. Rates from Shanghai to New York climbed 11% to $7,902, indicating significant industry stress.

events.title: GENMA supplies RTGs for Fujairah Port expansion
events.description: GENMA has secured an order to supply eight rubber-tyred gantry (RTG) cranes for the Port of Fujairah in the UAE, supporting AD Ports Group's terminal expansion. The cranes will be customized for the region's harsh environmental conditions.

events.title: Mundra Port Requires Prior Acceptance for Vessels
events.description: Prior acceptance is required for vessels calling at Mundra Port, India, effective 03 Jul 2026. Without approval, vessel call notifications (VCNs) will not be processed, except for container vessels under certain conditions.

events.title: Iran rejects Hormuz Strait demining plan
events.description: Iran has rejected a demining proposal from France and Oman for the Strait of Hormuz, which could heighten tensions and disrupt critical global oil shipments through the region.

events.title: Fire on Bulker in Gdansk Port
events.description: A large fire broke out on a bulker loading scrap metal at the Port of Gdansk, Poland, prompting a significant firefighting response.

events.title: MSC revises Britannia service rotation
events.description: MSC has revised the rotation of its Britannia service, removing calls at Zeebrugge, Mundra, and the westbound call at Colombo. The updated route now includes Felixstowe, Rotterdam, Antwerp, Gdansk, Gdynia, Klaipeda, Bremerhaven, and London.

events.title: China urges unimpeded Hormuz passage amid fee discussions
events.description: China has called for the unhindered flow of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as reports emerge that European powers may accept payment demands from Iran and Oman for vessel passage.

events.title: Japan Considering Iranian Oil Resumption Amid Shipping Risks
events.description: Japan is considering resuming Iranian oil purchases under a U.S. sanctions waiver, but buyers are seeking extended waivers and assurances about ship safety amid ongoing shipping risks.

events.title: DP World upgrades Port of Tartous with new cranes
events.description: DP World has delivered the first of three new Mobile Harbour Cranes to the Port of Tartous, Syria, as part of an $800 million investment to modernise the port and increase cargo-handling capacity by 40%.

events.title: German rail freight declines in 2025
events.description: Germany's rail freight sector declined by 5.4% in 2025 due to weaker industrial activity and DB Cargo restructuring, according to the Federal Network Agency.

events.title: Port of Maputo implements first Port Community System
events.description: The Port of Maputo has selected Kalé Logistics Solutions to develop Mozambique's first Port Community System, aiming to enhance digital transformation and trade efficiency in the region.

events.title: New container terminal development at Lach Huyen Port
events.location: Lach Huyen Port, Hai Phong, Vietnam
events.description: PSA Vietnam and Lach Huyen International Logistics & Industrial Park (LHF) have signed an agreement to jointly develop and operate four deep-sea container berths at Lach Huyen Port in Hai Phong, northern Vietnam.

events.title: Increased cargo theft risk during US holiday
events.description: Reduced staffing and freight activity over the US Independence Day holiday are creating opportunities for organized crime to target cargo, according to Verisk CargoNet. Theft activity historically peaks on July 3.

events.title: New vessels deployed to Asia-Latin America routes
events.description: Shipping lines are deploying newly delivered vessels, including the methanol-enabled CMA CGM Copper, to the Asia-Latin America trade lane. This reflects increased capacity and focus on the Asia-West Coast South America route.

events.title: India-LatAm freight space shortage disrupts exports
events.description: Indian exporters to Latin America face major supply chain disruptions due to reduced container space as carriers reallocate capacity for the early Asia peak season, causing delays and rising freight rates.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd acquires stake in Hamburg terminal CTH
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd's subsidiary Hanseatic Global Terminals is acquiring a 20% stake in Eurogate's Hamburg terminal CTH, potentially signaling a strategic industry move that could lead to a future takeover of Eurogate.

events.title: Container carriers raise rates on key east-west routes
events.description: Container carriers have introduced rate hikes on major east-west trades, causing a resurgence in spot freight rates during peak season. The increases follow a previous round of price announcements that led to significant week-on-week rate growth.

events.title: Jeddah port congestion disrupts Gulf land bridge
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd has suspended bookings into Jeddah due to severe congestion, with 5km truck queues reported. The surge is linked to increased use of the Gulf land bridge, despite recent diplomatic developments in the region.

events.title: Qatar LNG shipments withheld, disrupting European supply
events.description: QatarEnergy has extended a force majeure notice, withholding additional LNG shipments to Italy due to war damage to energy facilities. This action signals ongoing market disruption despite the Strait of Hormuz being open.

events.title: UK Announces New Sanctions on Russia's Maritime Transport
events.description: The UK Government announced a new sanctions package on 16 June 2026 targeting Russia’s maritime transport and energy exports, with a focus on disrupting its 'shadow fleet' and sanctions circumvention networks.

events.title: Hormuz sanctions risk highlighted by OFAC warning
events.description: On 1 May 2026, OFAC issued a warning that US and non-US operators could face sanctions risks for payments related to the Strait of Hormuz. The alert emphasizes that sanctions exposure may begin before any payment for safe passage is made.

events.title: Humanitarian crisis on hijacked tanker MT HONOUR 25
events.description: The humanitarian situation aboard the hijacked Republic of Palau-flagged tanker MT HONOUR 25 is rapidly deteriorating, according to the Chair of the Djibouti Code of Conduct/Jeddah Amendment. The vessel is currently in the Gulf of Aden, raising concerns for the safety and wellbeing of those onboard.

events.title: Typhoon Threatens Northern Marianas Islands
events.description: A strengthening typhoon is approaching the Northern Marianas Islands, prompting the U.S. Coast Guard to secure assets and issue storm warnings, which could disrupt maritime routes and operations in the region.

events.title: DP World invests in Port of Tartous with new cranes
events.description: DP World has delivered the first of three new Mobile Harbour Cranes to the Port of Tartous in Syria as part of a 30-year concession agreement with the Syrian government. Two additional cranes are expected by August, marking a step in the port's modernisation.

events.title: Venezuelan Port Closure Due to Earthquake
events.description: A major port on Venezuela's central coastline remains closed following destructive earthquakes that have disrupted normal operations and damaged infrastructure.

events.title: US-Iran technical talks near Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Parallel developments in the Strait of Hormuz's post-ceasefire environment, including US-Iran technical talks, suggest potential for renewed tension that could disrupt shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Norwegian rig workers avert strike with last-minute pay deal
events.location: Norwegian offshore oil and gas fields
events.description: A last-minute wage agreement between Norwegian unions and employers averted a potential strike that would have impacted over 600 workers on offshore drilling rigs and production platforms. The deal was reached just before a midnight deadline.

events.title: Hostages held on hijacked tanker Honour 25 off Somalia
events.description: The Palau-flagged tanker Honour 25, hijacked on April 24, remains under pirate control with 17 crew members held captive for over two months. Conditions for hostages are reported to be worsening.

events.title: PSA develops new deepsea container hub in Vietnam
events.description: PSA Vietnam has signed an agreement to invest in Lach Huyen Port to develop four deepsea container berths, with a projected annual handling capacity of 4.5 million teu. The project will be developed in partnership with Lach Huyen International Logistics & Industrial Park.

events.title: Shipping uncertainty in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz is resuming, but remains disrupted due to uncertainty over routing and control. Iran claims authority over traffic management, while the US disputes this, causing operational challenges for operators.

events.title: Container spot rates reach four-year high
events.description: Container spot freight rates have surged to four-year highs, driven by cargo frontloading due to tariffs and ongoing disruptions near the Strait of Hormuz. The Drewry World Container Index rose 9% week-on-week to $4,530 per 40ft container.

events.title: Peru court grants oversight of Chinese-built port
events.description: A Peruvian court has granted the government oversight of the Chinese-built Callao Port, reversing a prior decision amid growing geopolitical tensions between China and the U.S.

events.title: Iran Threatens Ships Using Non-Iranian Routes Through Hormuz
events.description: Iran has threatened to attack ships using non-Iranian designated routes through the Strait of Hormuz, amid internal leadership disagreements over priorities.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz traffic rebounding
events.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is gradually increasing following recent tensions between the US and Iran, leading to a noticeable easing of shipping costs. Reports indicate 40 vessels passed through the critical waterway on Monday.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic rebound
events.description: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has rebounded, raising concerns about oil market oversupply and prompting OCBC to lower its oil price forecasts through 2026.

events.title: EU proposal risks renewable shipping fuel supply
events.description: A proposed change to EU sustainability certification rules could disrupt the supply of bioLNG and eLNG to ships by threatening the use of liquefaction by equivalence. These fuels are key for decarbonizing shipping and are compatible with existing LNG infrastructure.

events.title: Somalia Piracy Hijacks Vessels, Endangering Crews
events.description: Three crews are being held by pirates in Somalia after their vessels were seized, with the International Maritime Organization reporting deteriorating conditions for the hijacked seafarers.

events.title: DP World opens logistics center in Sokhna
events.description: DP World has launched Egypt's first fully integrated Logistics Distribution Centre at Sokhna Logistics Park, enhancing regional trade capacity and reinforcing the Suez Canal Economic Zone's role as a trade gateway.

events.title: Port of Tokyo foreign container traffic declines 0.8% in March
events.description: The Port of Tokyo handled 353,773 TEUs of foreign-trade containers in March, a 0.8% decline compared to March 2023. Loaded containers fell 5%, while empty containers rose 13%.

events.title: Idaho bans non-domiciled CDL drivers
events.description: Idaho has eliminated non-domiciled commercial driver’s licenses, impacting trucking operations that rely on drivers from other states.

events.title: Increased Oil Flows Through Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz has increased to over 10 million barrels per day, supported by U.S. military efforts, reducing Iran's influence over oil flows in the region.

events.title: Europe targets shadow fleet tankers flying false Cameroon flag
events.description: Europe is taking action against tankers falsely using Cameroon's flag to transport Russian oil, including boarding vessels at sea. The move targets a shadow fleet circumventing sanctions.

events.title: Shadow Fleet Tanker Released After Month-Long Detention in France
events.description: French prosecutors and the Maritime Court in Brest reached a plea deal to fine and release the shadow fleet tanker Tagor after it was detained for a month. The detention caused a disruption in the vessel's operations and raised concerns about compliance and enforcement in the maritime sector.

events.title: Chittagong terminal raises LCL cargo storage fees
events.description: The Red Sea Gateway Terminal (RSGT) at Chittagong's Patenga Container Terminal has quadrupled storage fees for LCL cargoes to discourage importers from stockpiling goods beyond the free storage period.

events.title: Boluda upgrades Tenerife terminal with new cranes
events.description: Boluda Shipping has expanded its terminal in Tenerife with two STS cranes and four hybrid RTG cranes as part of a €40 million modernization plan, enhancing the terminal's operational capacity.

events.title: Asia-US ocean container rates exceed $7,900
events.description: Asia-US ocean container shipping rates have surpassed $7,900 due to peak-season demand, indicating significant industry pressure despite eased concerns over the Hormuz Strait.

events.title: Peru court orders oversight of Chinese-owned Chancay Port
events.description: A Peruvian court ruled that the government must oversee operations at the Chinese-owned Chancay Port near Lima, potentially impacting Beijing's influence in the region and affecting port policy and management.

events.title: Global container rates rise 9% due to tightened capacity
events.description: Global container spot rates increased by 9% as seasonal demand and reduced capacity strained key trade lanes, particularly the Transpacific and Asia-Europe routes. The rise reflects ongoing tightness in freight markets and potential supply chain bottlenecks.

events.title: MSC updates freight rates from Far East
events.description: MSC has announced new Freight All Kinds (FAK) base rates for shipments from the Far East to Northern Europe, the Mediterranean, North Africa, and the Black Sea, effective 15 July 2026 until 31 July 2026.

events.title: CORCA Bill Moves to Senate Over Cargo Theft Debate
events.description: The bipartisan CORCA bill, aimed at addressing cargo theft, has advanced to the Senate. Debate continues over whether federal intervention is justified, with concerns about underreported losses in the current system.

events.title: HMM Namu damaged in Hormuz projectile attack
events.description: The HMM Namu, a container ship damaged in a projectile attack in the Strait of Hormuz, is expected to leave the strait this month after repairs. The vessel remains one of two South Korean-owned ships stranded in the area.

events.title: Dover warns EES passport chaos disrupts UK-EU freight
events.description: Dover Harbour Board warns that the EU’s new Entry Exit Scheme is causing passport processing delays, threatening to disrupt UK-EU freight traffic as the summer holiday peak approaches.

events.title: Saudi Aramco increases oil exports via Ras Tanura
events.description: Saudi Aramco is increasing oil exports from Ras Tanura, with five supertankers carrying 10 million barrels recently exiting the Strait of Hormuz. The company is using spot pricing to accelerate sales to Asian markets.

events.title: Arctic LNG carrier maintenance in Denmark
events.description: The Arc7 icebreaking LNG carrier Rudolf Samoylovich has arrived at Denmark’s Fayard A/S Odense yard for maintenance, underscoring the yard’s critical role in servicing Russia’s Arctic LNG fleet, which supports year-round exports from the Yamal LNG project.

events.title: Russian refinery attacks cause fuel shortages and price hikes
events.description: Drone strikes on Russian oil refineries have led to gasoline shortages and a 1.7% rise in fuel prices over June 23-29, according to the Federal Statistics Service. This marks the largest weekly increase in at least 20 years.

events.title: Increased Suez Canal Container Ship Transits
events.description: The number of containerships transiting via the Suez Canal reached 35 in the week ended 28 June, the highest since January, according to the Drewry Red Sea Diversion Tracker. This increase suggests a potential shift away from Red Sea diversions amid ongoing geopolitical tensions.

events.title: Hapag-Lloyd revises AL3 service port rotation
events.description: Hapag-Lloyd is updating the port rotation for its AL3 service between Northern Europe and North America, effective July 1, 2026. The revised route includes calls at Southampton, Antwerp, Rotterdam, Hamburg, Saint John, Charleston, Savannah, and Norfolk.

events.title: Pirates board tanker south of Yemen
events.location: Gulf of Aden, south of Yemen
events.description: Armed pirates boarded a tanker approximately 76 nautical miles south of Balhaf, Yemen, in the Gulf of Aden. The crew stopped the vessel after being overpowered by the pirates, who used RPGs and other weapons.

events.title: GPS interference disrupts shipping near California
events.description: GPS interference impacted multiple vessels approaching the Port of Long Beach, raising concerns about navigation reliability and potential disruptions to global shipping routes.

events.title: House Calls for End to Jones Act Waiver
events.description: Republican House leaders have urged the White House to end the 150-day Jones Act waiver, which allows foreign-flagged vessels to transport fuel to Puerto Rico. The waiver was implemented to aid recovery after Hurricane Fiona but has drawn criticism from lawmakers who argue it undermines domestic shipping interests.

events.title: CMA CGM Acquires FedEx Supply Chain for US$1.4 Billion
events.description: CMA CGM has agreed to acquire FedEx Supply Chain, a US$1.4 billion deal that will nearly triple CEVA Logistics' contract logistics operations in North America.

events.title: Norwegian offshore unions in critical wage talks to avert strike
events.description: Unions representing over 600 offshore oil and gas workers in Norway are in state-mediated wage negotiations to prevent a potential strike. The dispute involves Styrke, SAFE, and DSO unions, with a failure to reach an agreement risking significant disruption to the sector.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz attention remains elevated
events.description: The Maritime Attention Index (MAI) indicates sustained high attention on the Strait of Hormuz, suggesting ongoing disruptions or tensions affecting shipping flows through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: Hormuz dispute shifts to control negotiations
events.description: Indirect talks between Washington and Tehran in Doha concluded with a temporary agreement to keep the Strait of Hormuz calm for seven days. Negotiations remain deadlocked over the terms of a previously signed memorandum of understanding.

events.title: Busan container rates rise sharply, pushing KCCI up 4.6%
events.description: Container spot rates from Busan have increased across most trade lanes, leading to a 4.62% rise in the KOBC Container Composite Index (KCCI), which now stands at 3,920 points.

events.title: Indian sailors avoid Gulf due to Middle East conflict
events.description: Indian seafarers, a major global workforce in merchant shipping, are hesitant to return to the Gulf amid the Middle East war, causing potential labor shortages and operational disruptions in shipping.

events.title: ARA bunker fuel availability tight
events.description: Bunker fuel availability is very tight in the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp (ARA) region, with buyers advised to book HSFO and VLSFO supplies up to seven days in advance to avoid high premiums.

events.title: Stranded ships in Persian Gulf decrease after evacuation
events.description: The number of container ships stranded in the Persian Gulf has dropped to 80 vessels (170,000 TEU) from a peak of 138 due to the US-Israel-Iran conflict. The International Maritime Organization's evacuation plan has helped more than 40% of ships escape.

events.title: EU imposes 3 euro customs fee on small parcel imports
events.description: Starting July 1, the EU is imposing a 3 euro customs fee on small parcel imports, prompting retailers to adjust systems for compliance and potentially affecting consumer behavior due to higher costs.

events.title: U.S. rail systems slow due to rising intermodal volume
events.description: Increased intermodal volume, driven by rising fuel and trucking costs, is causing delays at major U.S. rail systems. The surge is impacting freight flow efficiency across the country.

events.title: USMCA renewal uncertainty impacts North American trade
events.description: The U.S. has rejected automatic renewal of the USMCA trade agreement, leading to annual negotiations and creating uncertainty for North America’s $1.5 trillion trading relationship.

events.title: US rail slowdowns due to intermodal volume surge
events.description: US intermodal rail volume increased by 10% last week, but the surge is causing major network slowdowns with train speeds at 10-20 month lows. This contrasts with declining volumes in Canada, highlighting growing operational challenges in North American rail freight.

events.title: Extreme Heat Impacts Reefer Capacity in Eastern US
events.description: Extreme heat across the Eastern US is causing reefer spot rates and tender rejections to reach near all-time highs, as companies shift from dry van to refrigerated transport to manage temperature-sensitive freight.

events.title: U.S. House GOP urges Jones Act waivers to expire
events.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republican leaders are urging President Trump to let Jones Act shipping waivers expire in August, which could impact domestic maritime freight operations.

events.title: Armed Boarders Damage Merchant Ship Off Yemen
events.description: A merchant vessel was illegally boarded and damaged by armed personnel off the coast of Yemen. The UK Maritime Trade Operations issued a fresh security warning following the incident.

events.title: Hong Kong Port Restricted Area for Special Event
events.description: A restricted area is in place off Stonecutters Island, Hong Kong, from July 2 to July 6, 2026, for a special event. Only government launches and event-related vessels are permitted.

events.title: Container ship runs aground in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A foreign container ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz while using an unapproved route, according to Iranian state TV. The incident highlights tensions over control of the strategically vital waterway.

events.title: Pirate Boarding of Bulk Carrier in Gulf of Aden
events.description: A bulk carrier was boarded by pirates armed with a rocket-propelled grenade launcher approximately 110 nautical miles northeast of Bosaso, Somalia. The incident occurred in the Internationally Recommended Transit Corridor.

events.title: Containership Grounding Near Hormuz Strait
events.description: A containership reportedly went aground near the Strait of Hormuz for operating outside its designated shipping lane. Iranian state media released a video and is using the incident to assert control over the critical waterway.

events.title: Russian warship orders Greenpeace away from sanctioned tanker
events.description: On June 30, a Russian warship ordered Greenpeace activists to stay away while they were photographing a sanctioned crude oil tanker, the Kira K, in the Black Sea. The incident highlights ongoing tensions and enforcement of sanctions affecting maritime operations.

events.title: Pirates Attack Ships in Gulf of Aden
events.description: A pirate group boarded and damaged a vessel on July 1 in the Gulf of Aden, then pursued a second ship. Activity in the region remains elevated.

events.title: Oman's Role in Future of Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran has been asserting control over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, but analysis shows Oman's position and decisions will also significantly influence the strait's future and regional freight flows.

events.title: Iran escalates control claims over Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Iran is escalating steps to assert control over the Strait of Hormuz, with strikes on transiting vessels and sites causing marine traffic disruptions. This comes amid ongoing US-Iran negotiations and rising oil volumes from Gulf states.

events.title: IATA Direct Air Waybill changes impact forwarders
events.description: IATA's revised Direct Air Waybill (DAWB) framework, effective immediately, alters contractual responsibilities between airlines, shippers, and forwarders, potentially increasing legal and insurance risks for freight forwarders. FIATA claims the industry review process was bypassed.

events.title: Foreign Ship Runs Aground in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: A foreign container ship ran aground in the Strait of Hormuz after taking an 'unauthorized' route, according to Iran. The incident highlights ongoing tensions and risks in this critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Russia imports gasoline from India to address shortages
events.description: Russia has begun importing gasoline from India via sea to address fuel shortages caused by Ukrainian attacks on its energy infrastructure.

events.title: US and Iran Hold Talks to Secure Hormuz Shipping
events.description: The U.S. and Iran held indirect technical talks in Doha to agree on the flow of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and work toward a lasting ceasefire.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz peace talks boost coal prices
events.description: Thermal coal prices fell as ongoing peace talks between the US and Iran raised hopes for a resolution and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint.

events.title: Port of La Guaira operations suspended after earthquake
events.description: Commercial operations at the Port of La Guaira in Venezuela remain suspended following a recent earthquake. Authorities have not yet authorized the resumption of import and export activities, causing shipment delays.

events.title: Truck driver steals $2.9M cargo using fake docs
events.description: An Indiana truck driver was arrested for allegedly using fraudulent documents to steal $2.9 million worth of tungsten oxide powder in Pennsylvania. The stolen cargo was intended for shipment to Japan.

events.title: Maersk cautious on Middle East transits due to ongoing instability
events.description: Maersk is proceeding with caution regarding Middle East transits due to ongoing unpredictability, despite recent positive developments between the US and Iran. The company will resume transits only after confirming safety through thorough risk assessments.

events.title: Rising freight costs from Brazil to US impact trade
events.description: Ocean spot rates from Brazil to the US have surged by 50% for 40ft containers, driven by higher fuel costs and front-loading to avoid upcoming US tariffs. This increase signals potential trade flow disruptions despite weak underlying demand.

events.title: CBP enforces strict forced labor import rules
events.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection has issued new forced labor enforcement guidelines requiring full documentation of every supplier in a production chain. Failure to provide this visibility may result in denied entry for imported goods, significantly impacting logistics and import operations.

events.title: MSC invests in Vizhinjam Port, raising monopoly concerns
events.description: MSC has agreed to acquire a 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port, part of Adani Ports, as the port undergoes a major expansion. This move raises concerns about market dominance and could influence freight flows in the region.

events.title: EU ends de minimis customs exemption for low-value imports
events.description: The EU has terminated the de minimis exemption for low-value imports, imposing a €3 customs duty on parcels under €150. This policy change aims to curb the influx of low-cost goods, particularly from China, and may impact cross-border e-commerce and logistics flows.

events.title: MSC orders 20 new ultra-large container vessels
events.description: MSC has placed orders and options for 20 ultra-large container vessels with the Hengli yard in China, increasing its capacity lead over Maersk and expanding its already massive newbuilding pipeline.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz closure disrupts global crude flows
events.description: The effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz in Q2 2026 has significantly disrupted global crude oil flows, forcing the VLCC market to adapt and reroute shipments away from this critical oil chokepoint.

events.title: COSCO wins Tarragona terminal award
events.description: A COSCO Shipping Ports-led consortium has received an award notification to develop a new multipurpose terminal at the Port of Tarragona, Spain. The project, valued at around €144.6m, could expand COSCO's European port network.

events.title: Middle East Disruptions Impact Cargo Flows
events.description: Ongoing disruptions in the Middle East are affecting cargo flows and routing decisions, causing shifts in supply chain operations across Asia-Pacific trade corridors.

events.title: Bunker Fuel Availability Strain in Singapore
events.description: VLSFO bunker fuel availability in Singapore remains under strain, with lead times varying from nine days to over four weeks across suppliers.

events.title: MSC invests $1.4B for 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port
events.description: Adani Ports has entered into a definitive agreement with Terminal Investment Limited (TiL), part of MSC Group, for the sale of a 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port for $1.4 billion. The investment signals a strategic industry move that could impact regional port operations and logistics flows.

events.title: Acid spill disrupts Houston Ship Channel operations
events.description: A sulfuric acid spill at a terminal on the Houston Ship Channel led to OSHA fining the operator $82,000. The incident disrupted port operations and raised safety concerns.

events.title: Ukraine warns ships aiding Russia in conflict
events.description: Ukraine has issued a warning to ships aiding Russia in the conflict, responding to Russian allegations of terrorism following strikes on commercial shipping. The letter was addressed to the International Maritime Organization (IMO).

events.title: Pirates Hold Tanker Crew Off Somalia
events.description: A tanker's crew has been held by pirates off the coast of Somalia since early May. Egypt is intensifying efforts to secure their release.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Crisis Economic Impact
events.description: The UN warns that despite the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, the over 100-day disruption has caused lasting economic damage to global shipping and energy markets.

events.title: Iran Asserts Control Over Hormuz Strait
events.description: Iran has escalated rhetoric about asserting control over maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint, ahead of new talks in Qatar aimed at ending its conflict with the US.

events.title: US Retailers Accelerate China Orders Ahead of Tariff Hike
events.description: U.S. retailers are frontloading orders from China by four-to-six weeks to secure holiday inventory before expected tariff hikes later this year, according to shipping executives.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Disruption Affects LNG Trade
events.description: Shipping disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran war could stall global liquefied natural gas trade in 2026, according to Shell, though growth is expected to resume in 2027.

events.title: AIS Protocol Update in Kuwaiti Waters
events.description: As of 30 Jun 2026, vessels in Kuwaiti waters must maintain continuous AIS operation. This policy change may affect vessel tracking and compliance procedures for ships transiting the area.

events.title: Maersk seasonal blank sailing on Northern Star service
events.description: Maersk has announced a seasonal blank sailing on its Northern Star service due to seasonal cargo demand and market fluctuations. The affected voyage includes a southbound departure from Shanghai on 6 September 2026.

events.title: Maersk adjusts intermodal fuel fees in Europe
events.description: Maersk has updated its Intermodal Fuel Fee (EFS/IFS) levels across Europe due to ongoing high energy prices and Middle East security issues affecting fuel availability. The new fees apply from 6 July to 20 July 2026 and will be reviewed every two weeks.

events.title: Containers lost at sea nearly triple in 2025
events.description: A 2025 report found that 1,478 containers were lost at sea, nearly tripling from previous years, primarily due to challenging weather and vessel fires.

events.title: U.S. cargo airlines seek Venezuela flight approval
events.description: Atlas Air and ABX Air have requested U.S. government approval to start cargo flights to Venezuela, which has been restricted from commercial service. This could signal a potential policy shift affecting freight access to the region.

events.title: Oman clarifies stance on Strait of Hormuz amid Iran talks
events.description: Oman has clarified its position on the Strait of Hormuz amid widening talks with Iran, following recent exchanges of fire between the U.S. and Iran. The situation remains tense, with potential implications for global shipping through this critical chokepoint.

events.title: MSC revises freight rates from South Asia to Europe
events.location: South Asia to Europe shipping routes
events.description: MSC has announced updated Freight All Kinds (FAK) rates for shipments from South Asia, including Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, India, and Pakistan, to Europe, effective 15 July 2026. The new rates are US$2,550 per 20DV and US$2,850 per 40DV/HC for Sri Lanka and Bangladesh.

events.title: COSCO consortium wins Tarragona terminal concession
events.description: A consortium led by COSCO SHIPPING Ports has been awarded a concession to develop a multipurpose terminal at the Port of Tarragona, Spain, following a public tender process.

events.title: Global Truck Driver Shortage Crisis
events.description: The global truck driver shortage, with nearly 3 million vacancies, is now a structural threat to freight transport, according to the International Road Transport Union. An aging workforce and growing demand are expected to worsen the crisis over the next five years.

events.title: Port congestion hits four-year high in North Asia
events.description: Container port congestion has reached a four-year high, with 3.7 million teu tied up globally. North Asian ports account for 38% of the congestion, leading to potential freight rate increases.

events.title: MSC gains stake in Vizhinjam port
events.description: MSC's terminal arm, TiL, acquires a 49% stake in Vizhinjam Port for $2.85 billion, strengthening its partnership with APSEZ.

events.title: Hormuz closure impacts fuel costs and BAFs
events.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has increased fuel costs, leading to new BAFs effective 1 July. Carriers are passing on these costs to shippers, causing concerns over rising freight expenses.

events.title: Russia extends oil export ban linked to G7/EU price cap
events.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin extended the ban on oil and petroleum product exports subject to the G7 and EU price cap through the end of 2027. The move could further disrupt global oil markets and trade flows.

events.title: U.S.-Iran military exchanges raise Hormuz concerns
events.description: Fresh military exchanges between the U.S. and Iran have raised concerns over potential supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil chokepoint. However, renewed diplomatic talks have tempered some of these fears, leading to a slight decline in oil prices.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz Gradually Reopening
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz is gradually reopening after disruptions, though freight rates continue to rise. Carriers are cancelling fewer sailings, indicating stabilizing schedules.

events.title: Hormuz Strait Tensions Persist Amid Peace Talks
events.description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains steady, but ongoing tensions between Iran and the U.S. continue to cast uncertainty. Peace talks are set to resume in Doha, Qatar, with little clarity on a resolution.

events.title: Adani and MSC expand partnership at Vizhinjam Port
events.description: Adani Ports and MSC have agreed for Terminal Investment Limited to acquire a 49% stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Private Limited, with a total deal value of US$2.85 billion. The partnership aims to develop and operate the Vizhinjam port, a strategic deep-water port on India's southwest coast.

events.title: Carriers boost capacity on East Asia-Australia route
events.description: Shipping lines are increasing capacity on the East Asia-Australia trade lane due to a 30% surge in freight rates. CMA CGM and Cosco affiliate OOCL are launching a dedicated express service to meet rising demand and address tight capacity.

events.title: Increased Traffic in Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has increased following Iran’s recent attacks on ships, with more crude tankers entering the Persian Gulf.

events.title: China Conducts Patrols Near Scarborough Shoal
events.description: China's military and coast guard conducted patrols around Scarborough Shoal in the South China Sea, asserting territorial claims and potentially heightening regional tensions.

events.title: U.S. dry van spot rates surge 31% YoY
events.description: The U.S. Bank Freight Payment Index shows dry van spot rates surged 31% year-over-year in May 2026 due to tightening capacity, signaling significant cost increases in freight transportation.

events.title: Russian LNG carrier armed with machine guns in Baltic Sea
events.description: A Russian LNG carrier operating in the Baltic Sea has been spotted with heavy machine guns mounted on board, marking an unusual militarization of a commercial vessel. The action raises concerns about the security implications for maritime trade routes in the region.

events.title: MSC acquires stake in Vizhinjam port
events.description: Mediterranean Shipping Company (MSC) has agreed to acquire a 49% stake in Adani Ports' Vizhinjam transhipment terminal in Kerala, India, for $1.4 billion. The investment signals MSC's growing commitment to India's container market and port infrastructure.

events.title: Yanbu Red Sea bypass remains active
events.description: The Red Sea bypass route through Yanbu in Saudi Arabia is operating at near-peak capacity as Gulf terminals reopen, with ongoing toll claims from Tehran and Muscat suggesting the alternative route may remain in use.

events.title: BNSF Expands Barstow Intermodal Hub
events.description: BNSF has won approval for a $4 billion intermodal facility in Barstow, aiming to shift container sorting away from Los Angeles. The project raises questions about costs, logistics efficiency, and potential impacts on freight flows.

events.title: Iran Conflict Drives Up BAF and Shipping Costs
events.description: The escalation of the Iran conflict has caused fuel-related charges (BAF) to more than double, with carriers imposing emergency bunker surcharges. Shippers now face increased transportation costs and the risk of being charged twice for fuel price increases.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz operational capacity severely reduced
events.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains open but is operating at about 10% of pre-war levels due to two kinetic incidents, a suspended IMO evacuation corridor, and ongoing disruptions. Commercial traffic is significantly constrained, impacting global freight flows.

events.title: AG Oil Flows Normalization Progress
events.description: Following a ceasefire on 24 June, temporary routing arrangements coordinated by Oman and the IMO have enabled a gradual resumption of commercial shipping in a previously contested transit regime.

events.title: Cargo ship taking on water along Indian Coast
events.description: A small cargo vessel along the Indian Coast reported taking on water in multiple compartments. The Indian Coast Guard provided assistance to the vessel.

events.title: SC Ports suspends Leatherman Terminal operations
events.location: Hugh K. Leatherman Terminal, South Carolina
events.description: South Carolina Ports will temporarily pause container operations at the Hugh K. Leatherman Terminal starting August 1 due to ongoing trade challenges and poor performance since the terminal's opening over five years ago.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz transit uncertainty after attacks
events.description: Ships continued to transit the Strait of Hormuz, but some owners reduced their visibility due to recent attacks, raising concerns about navigation safety in the critical oil shipping corridor.

events.title: Russian LNG Vessel Armed with Machine Guns
events.description: Estonian surveillance detected machine guns on a Russian LNG vessel, marking a potential escalation in the militarization of commercial shipping.

events.title: Maersk shifts SoCal imports to UP from BNSF
events.description: Maersk is shifting import containers moving inland from Southern California to Union Pacific (UP) from BNSF, signaling a major rail route adjustment affecting freight flows.

events.title: Top container line invests in Hamburg terminal
events.description: A top-five container line is acquiring a stake in Hamburg's Eurogate terminal, signaling a strategic industry move that could influence European port operations and logistics flows.
